title: Definitions
content: In this Chapter, unless there is anything repugnant in the subject or context: (a) 'adult' means a person who has attained the age of eighteen years; (b) 'arsh' means the compensation specified in this Chapter to be paid to the victim or his heirs; (c) 'daman' means the compensation determined by the Court to be paid by the offender to the victim for causing hurt not liable to arsh; (d) 'diyat' means the compensation specified in Section 323 to be paid to the heirs of the victim...

footnotes.text: Financial Remedies: Arsh is a statutorily fixed compensation amount for specific injuries, whereas Daman is left to the judicial discretion of the court based on medical expenses and suffering.

title: Qatl-i-Amd
content: Whoever, with the intention of causing death or with the intention of causing bodily injury to a person, by doing an act which in the ordinary course of nature is likely to cause death, or with the knowledge that his act is so imminently dangerous that it must in all probability cause death, causes the death of such person, is said to commit qatl-i-amd.

illumination_lamp.scenario: A intentionally shoots B with a firearm at a vital part of the body, and B dies as a result.
illumination_lamp.legal_effect: A has committed Qatl-i-Amd under Section 300.

footnotes.text: Intentional Homicide: This replaces the old colonial concept of 'Culpable Homicide amounting to Murder'. The focus is directly on the specific intent to cause death or bodily injury likely to cause death in the ordinary course of nature.

title: Punishment of qatl-i-amd
content: Whoever commits qatl-i-amd shall, subject to the provisions of this Chapter, be: (a) punished with death as qisas; (b) punished with death or imprisonment for life as ta'zir having regard to the facts and circumstances of the case; or (c) punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to twenty-five years, where according to Injunctions of Islam the punishment of qisas is not applicable.

footnotes.text: Three-Tiered Sentencing: Clause (a) applies if proof standards under Islamic law (like voluntary confession or specific eyewitness testimony) are met. Clause (b) is the standard statutory option used by state courts under Ta'zir.

title: Qatl Shibh-i-Amd
content: Whoever, with intent to cause harm to the body or mind of any person, causes the death of that or of any other person by means of a weapon or an act which in the ordinary course of nature is not likely to cause death, is said to commit qatl shibh-i-amd.

sentencing_ladder.penalty: Diyat and up to 25 years imprisonment as ta'zir

illumination_lamp.scenario: A intentionally strikes B with a small stick, not intending to kill, but B unexpectedly dies due to an underlying health condition.
illumination_lamp.legal_effect: A is guilty of Qatl Shibh-i-Amd under Section 315.

footnotes.text: Manslaughter / Quasi-Intentional Homicide: The offender intended to cause harm or minor injury (e.g., striking someone with a light stick), but unexpectedly caused their death.

title: Qatl-i-Khata
content: Whoever, without any intention to cause the death of, or cause harm to, a person, causes the death of such person by mistake of act or by mistake of fact, is said to commit qatl-i-khata.

sentencing_ladder.penalty: Liability defaults to paying Diyat to the heirs

media_tutor.title: Offences Affecting the Human Body - Legal Analysis

footnotes.text: Accidental Homicide: For example, shooting at a target or animal but accidentally striking a hidden bystander. There is zero criminal intent to harm.
